name: Curry Masala Gravy
recipeIngredient: Garlic Puree (5 tbsp, equivalent to 5 portions)
Ginger Puree (4 tbsp, equivalent to 4 portions)
Onion Puree (2 1/2 pint, equivalent to 10 portions)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the spices with enough water to make a paste that is thin enough to spread like catsup. Allow it to stand.
2. Heat about 5 tablespoons of oil in a saucepan that holds between 6 and 8 pints. Stir-fry the garlic puree for 30 seconds, then add the ginger puree and cook for another 30 seconds. Add another 5 tablespoons of oil and when hot, add the spice mixture and stir-fry for another 30 seconds. Add the remaining oil and onion puree, stirring gently until well combined for about 10 minutes.
3. Blend the canned tomatoes into a smooth puree, then add it to the pan along with the tomato paste. Mix thoroughly and add enough water to achieve a medium-thick soup consistency. Add sugar and salt to taste.
4. Simmer gently for at least 30 minutes but no more than 1 hour, reducing until the mixture reaches a texture similar to applesauce that is still pourable. If it becomes too dry, add a small amount of water.
5. Divide the gravy into 10 equal portions and freeze in small freezer containers. This mild base can be used as a substitute for individual garlic, ginger, and onion purees in various recipes, allowing you to adjust with additional spices as desired.
Average yield: Approximately 1/2 pint
